rationale.R1.2: The concluding paragraph makes a broad inferential leap — from PIT uniformity and 68% coverage on one held-out set to recommending unrestricted use of DEEP-z posteriors across the full LSST 3x2pt pipeline without per-bin recalibration. Checking that premises support conclusions is centrally expected under any serious reading.
rationale.R1.3: Numerous quantitative claims (bias < 0.001, σ_NMAD = 0.018, outlier fraction 1.4%, 5σ bootstrap result, CRPS dominance) appear inline without referencing tables, figures, or appendices. A careful look requires assessing whether stated evidence actually grounds these claims.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' is the canonical lightweight delegation; prose quality, flow, and phrasing review are co-centrally expected alongside technical scrutiny.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I prioritize prose-level writing quality (clarity, flow, sentence phrasing), technical scrutiny of the claims and their logical/evidential support, or both with equal weight?
